Exploring the Pros and Cons of Netflix’s Latest Experiment in Streaming
The New Face of Streaming: Always-On Channels
In an unexpected move, Netflix has begun experimenting with always-on channels. Picture traditional television with scheduled shows, but on a streaming platform. Intriguing, right? As a frequent user of streaming services, I couldn’t resist this nostalgia-tinged innovation.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Stefan Coders on PexelsFirst Impressions: What’s Old is New Again
When I started Netflix’s always-on channels, it felt like returning to a time when “What’s On?” was a real question, not just a casual scroll through an endless library. Instead of controlling every viewing choice, we are invited to embrace the curated wisdom of the algorithm. It’s an interesting proposition, especially for those who battle decision fatigue.
The first channel I sampled was filled with iconic ’90s sitcoms. Despite having watched “Friends” multiple times, the familiarity was comforting, and I found myself engaged. The biggest draw? It brings both unexpected joy and a break from having to make choices.
Pros: Convenience and Discovery
